data-dictionary-013.quickRevisionNotes:
What: DDIC mistakes — missing MANDT, wrong delivery class, no TMG, skipping WHERE-USED before changes — cause production failures, transport errors, and data corruption.

Used for:
- Validating custom tables have MANDT as first key (client-dependent design)
- Checking delivery class matches data nature (A vs C vs G)
- Running WHERE-USED before renaming or deleting any DDIC object

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- DD02L (tables), DD03L (fields), DD12L (indexes), TDDAT (TMG registration)
- SE11 -> Where-Used; SE54 for TMG; SM30 for maintenance dialog
- contflag A/C/G/L/S/E identifies delivery class

Interview keyword: MANDT first, WHERE-USED always, delivery class matters.

followupAnswerBank.question: What happens if you reduce a field length in a table that has data?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Field length kam karna high-risk operation hai. SE14 database conversion existing data ko smaller field mein fit karne ki try karega — new length se exceed karne wala data truncate hoga ya agar SAP potential data loss detect kare toh conversion error ke saath fail ho sakti hai. Yeh field internal table definitions mein use karne wale saare ABAP programs ka type mismatch hoga agar unka internal structure abhi bhi old length rakhta hai — yeh runtime dumps cause karta hai. Safe approach: WHERE-USED run karo, saare programs check karo, internal table definitions update karo, pehle development mein SE14 conversion test karo, verify karo ki data new length mein fit hota hai, phir transport karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Reducing a field length is a high-risk operation. SE14 database conversion will attempt to fit existing data into the smaller field — data that exceeds the new length will be truncated or the conversion may fail with an error if SAP detects potential data loss. All ABAP programs that use this field in internal table definitions will have a type mismatch if their internal structure still has the old length — this causes runtime dumps. The safe approach: run WHERE-USED, check all programs, update internal table definitions, test SE14 conversion in development first, verify data fits in new length, then transport.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Incident: Customer reference field ZREF reduced from CHAR 30 to CHAR 20 due to 'business requirement'. SE14 conversion in development: successful (dev data all under 20 chars). Production SE14: failed — 847 records had reference data > 20 chars. Emergency rollback needed. Lesson: always check actual data length distribution before reducing field length.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Dev system mein SE14 conversion successful dekh ke assume karna production bhi okay hoga. Dev data aur production data alag hote hain. Production pe data length analysis karo: SELECT MAX(LENGTH(zref)) FROM zcustom_table BEFORE reducing field length.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Before reducing field length - check actual data length:
SELECT MAX( strlen( zref_field ) ) AS max_len,
       COUNT( * )                  AS total_recs
  FROM zcustom_table
  INTO @DATA(ls_check).

IF ls_check-max_len > 20.  " New intended length
  WRITE: / 'Data exists longer than new field length!'.
  WRITE: / 'Max length:', ls_check-max_len.
ENDIF.
" Only proceed with SE14 conversion if max_len <= new length

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you correctly set up a custom table for SM30 maintenance?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Custom table ke liye SM30 maintenance enable karne ke liye: pehle SE11 mein table create aur activate karo. Phir SE11 mein, table open karo, aur Utilities → Table Maintenance Generator par navigate karo. Function group specify karo (new custom create karo ya existing use karo), access control ke liye authorization group set karo, maintenance type choose karo — simple tables ke liye One Step, header-item relationships ke liye Two Step. Generate click karo. SAP automatically Dynpro programs aur function modules generate karta hai. Phir SM30 → table name enter karo → maintenance form available hai. Bina TMG ke, SM30 'No maintenance dialog defined' error dikhata hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: To enable SM30 maintenance for a custom table: first create and activate the table in SE11. Then go to SE11, open the table, and navigate to Utilities → Table Maintenance Generator. Specify a function group (create a new custom one or use existing), set an authorization group for access control, choose maintenance type — One Step for simple tables, Two Step for header-item relationships. Click Generate. SAP generates Dynpro programs and function modules automatically. Then SM30 → enter table name → maintenance form is available. Without TMG, SM30 shows 'No maintenance dialog defined' error.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Shortcut mistake: Created custom config table ZCFG_PLANT. Went to SM30, entered table name — error: no maintenance dialog. Had to go back to SE11, run TMG, generate maintenance dialog, then transport the generated programs along with the table. TMG generates multiple objects — function group, function modules, screens — all need to be in the transport.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: TMG run karne ke baad sirf table transport karna — TMG generated programs transport bhool jaana. TMG creates function group and programs — sab objects collect karo transport request mein warna target system mein SM30 again fails.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE11: Table Maintenance Generator setup
" Menu: Utilities → Table Maintenance Generator
" Function Group: ZFG_ZCFG_PLANT (new, Z-prefix)
" Authorization Group: &NC& (no auth check) or ZCFG
" Maintenance Type: One step (simple table)
" Generate → Multiple objects created

" Objects generated (all need transport):
" Function Group: ZFG_ZCFG_PLANT
" Function Modules: within the function group
" Dynpro Screens: maintenance screens

" Test via SM30:
" SM30 → Table/View: ZCFG_PLANT → Maintain

followupAnswerBank.question: What are the risks of incorrect table buffering settings?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Incorrect buffering ke do risk directions hain. Frequently changed table over-buffer karne se stale data hota hai: buffer purane values hold karta hai jabki database mein newer data hai — programs stale values par wrong decisions lete hain, aur bug intermittent aur diagnose karna hard hota hai. Stable small lookup table under-buffer karne se database round trips waste hote hain — minor but measurable performance impact. Teesra risk: wrong buffer type. Full buffering entire table load karti hai — badi tables ke liye unsuitable. Generic buffering correct generic key fields require karti hai — wrong configuration ya toh keys ke across data leakage ya buffer misses cause kar sakti hai. Review: sirf woh tables buffer karo jo infrequently change hoti hain aur frequently read hoti hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Incorrect buffering has two risk directions. Over-buffering a frequently changed table causes stale data: the buffer holds old values while the database has newer data — programs make wrong decisions based on stale values, and the bug is intermittent and hard to diagnose. Under-buffering a stable small lookup table wastes database round trips — minor but measurable performance impact. A third risk: wrong buffer type. Full buffering loads entire table — unsuitable for large tables. Generic buffering requires correct generic key fields — wrong configuration can cause either data leakage across keys or buffer misses. Review: only buffer tables that change infrequently and are read frequently.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Stale data bug: Price config table ZCFG_PRICE had full buffering. Business updated price — system continued showing old price for 10 minutes until buffer expired. Complaints from business. Root cause: table was updated frequently (multiple times per day) but was fully buffered with 600-second timeout. Fix: removed buffering — HANA fast enough for direct read, no stale data issue.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Ek table ko 'performance ke liye' buffer karna without checking change frequency. Always ask: kitni baar ye table change hoti hai per day? Agar zyada baar — do not buffer. Agar rarely — buffer is fine.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE11: Check and set buffering
" Table Properties → Technical Settings
" Buffering: Not Allowed / Allowed not active / Fully Buffered
"            Partially Buffered / Single Records

" Detect stale buffer issue (force buffer refresh):
CALL FUNCTION 'BUFFER_REFRESH_REQUIRED'
  EXPORTING
    table_name = 'ZCFG_PRICE'.

" Or use ABAP statement to bypass buffer:
SELECT SINGLE *
  FROM zcfg_price BYPASSING BUFFER
  INTO @DATA(ls_price)
  WHERE zkey = @lv_key.

followupAnswerBank.question: Why is the MANDT field so important and what happens if it is missing?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: MANDT field client identifier hai — multiple clients (development, QA, production) chalane wale SAP system mein, client-dependent tables data ko per client isolate karne ke liye MANDT ko first key field use karte hain. SAP automatically client-dependent tables par saare SELECT statements mein WHERE MANDT = SY-MANDT append karta hai, toh client 300 sirf client 300 data dekhta hai. Agar MANDT custom table se missing hai, table client-independent ban jati hai — saare clients same data share karte hain. Development test data production mein visible hota hai, production data inadvertently developers ke through modify ho sakta hai, aur client-level security break ho jati hai. Yeh design error hai jo table mein data already aane ke baad fix karna very costly hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The MANDT field is the client identifier — in a SAP system running multiple clients (development, QA, production), client-dependent tables use MANDT as the first key field to isolate data per client. SAP automatically appends WHERE MANDT = SY-MANDT to all SELECT statements on client-dependent tables, so client 300 sees only client 300 data. If MANDT is missing from a custom table, the table becomes client-independent — all clients share the same data. Development test data is visible in production, production data can be inadvertently modified by developers, and client-level security is broken. This is a design error that is very costly to fix after data is already in the table.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Audit finding: During S/4HANA readiness audit, discovered 3 custom tables were missing MANDT field. All 3 contained configuration data that was the same in all clients — initially seemed harmless. But one table had dev-specific test entries visible in production. Fix required: create new tables with MANDT, migrate data, update all programs — 2 weeks of rework for 3 tables.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Client-independent table intentionally banana without documenting why. If truly cross-client data needed, document the decision explicitly. But if client isolation is needed — MANDT must be first key field. No exceptions.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Correct: Client-dependent custom table structure
" SE11: Table ZCUSTOM_CLIENT_DEP
" Field  Type   Len  Key  Description
" MANDT  CLNT   3    X    Client (auto-filtered)
" ZKEY1  CHAR   10   X    Custom Key 1
" ZVAL1  CHAR   50        Custom Value

" Wrong: Missing MANDT - client-independent by accident
" ZKEY1  CHAR   10   X    (no isolation between clients)
" ZVAL1  CHAR   50

" Verify MANDT is first key field:
SELECT fieldname, position, keyflag
  FROM dd03l WHERE tabname = 'ZCUSTOM_T' AND as4local = 'A'
  ORDER BY position.

followupAnswerBank.question: What problems occur when an append structure is not activated?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Create kiya gaya but activate nahi kiya gaya append structure underlying database table modify nahi karta. Append mein defined fields inactive state mein hain — yeh ABAP Dictionary metadata mein exist karte hain but database par apply nahi hue. Inn fields ko reference karne wale ABAP programs issues face karenge: fields database table structure mein nahi hain, toh SELECT empty return karega ya error karega, aur append fields reference karne wale internal table definitions type check warnings ya runtime errors cause kar sakte hain. Fix: SE11 mein append structure open karo aur Activate click karo (ya SE11 utilities mein mass activation use karo). Activation ke baad, physical database table alter karne ke liye SE14 run karna pad sakta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: An append structure created but not activated does not modify the underlying database table. The fields defined in the append are in an inactive state — they exist in ABAP Dictionary metadata but have not been applied to the database. ABAP programs referencing these fields will face issues: the fields are not in the database table structure, so SELECT will return empty or error, and internal table definitions referencing the append fields may cause type check warnings or runtime errors. Fix: Open the append structure in SE11 and click Activate (or use mass activation in SE11 utilities). After activation, SE14 may need to run to physically alter the database table.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Testing issue: Developer added append structure ZZ1_VBAK with field ZCUSTOM_REF. Program SELECT tried to read ZCUSTOM_REF from VBAK — always came empty even after INSERT. Investigation: SE11 showed append in inactive status (orange light). Activated the append, ran SE14 to convert database table — after activation, field worked correctly. Root cause: forgot to activate after creation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: SE11 mein object save karna aur activate karna ko same samajhna. Save = metadata saved as inactive. Activate = applied to database, available in ABAP. Always activate after every change in SE11.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Check activation status of append:
SELECT tabname, as4local
  FROM dd02l
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_status)
  WHERE tabname = 'ZZ1_VBAK'.
" as4local: 'A' = Active, 'M' = Modified/Inactive

" If inactive, activate via SE11 or:
" Mass activation: SE11 → Edit → Activate All

" After activation, verify field in database:
SELECT COUNT( * )
  FROM vbak
  WHERE zcustom_ref IS NOT INITIAL
  INTO @DATA(lv_count).
" If field activated correctly, this SELECT works

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the impact of deleting a fixed value from a domain?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Domain se fixed value delete karne ke kayi impacts hain. Pehle, value F4 search help mein ab appear nahi hoti — users new entries ke liye select nahi kar sakte. Doosre, domain values ke against koi bhi validation check deleted value reject karega — domain value checks use karne wale programs uss value wale existing records ko invalid flag karenge. Teesre, deleted value contain karne wale existing database records unchanged rehte hain — data delete nahi hota, but ab technically valid value set ke bahar hai. Yeh inconsistent data create karta hai: records exist karte hain jo value domain ab valid recognize nahi karta. Hamesha deletion se pehle value ke usage ke liye data check karo, aur existing records ko migration ya data cleansing step ke saath handle karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Deleting a fixed value from a domain has several impacts. First, the value no longer appears in F4 search help — users cannot select it for new entries. Second, any validation check against domain values will reject the deleted value — programs using domain value checks will flag existing records with that value as invalid. Third, existing database records containing the deleted value remain unchanged — the data is not deleted, but it is now technically outside the valid value set. This creates inconsistent data: records exist with a value the domain no longer recognizes as valid. Always check data for usage of the value before deletion, and handle existing records with a migration or data cleansing step.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Configuration change: Status value 'HOLD' removed from domain ZORDER_STATUS fixed values — business said status no longer used. But 1,200 orders in database had status 'HOLD'. After domain change, report showing order status count showed 1,200 records with 'invalid' status. Programs doing status validation failed for those orders. Emergency fix: domain value 'HOLD' reinstated as deprecated, data migration run to change 'HOLD' orders to new valid status.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Domain se fixed value delete karna without checking if existing database data uses that value. Always query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM table WHERE zfield = 'HOLD' before deleting domain fixed value. If count > 0, data migration required first.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Before deleting domain fixed value 'HOLD':
" Step 1: Check data usage
SELECT COUNT( * ) AS cnt
  FROM zcustom_order
  INTO @DATA(lv_cnt)
  WHERE zstatus = 'HOLD'.

IF lv_cnt > 0.
  WRITE: / lv_cnt, 'records use HOLD status.'.
  WRITE: / 'Migrate data before deleting domain value!'.
ENDIF.

" Step 2: Only delete domain value if lv_cnt = 0
" Or first migrate: UPDATE zcustom_order SET zstatus = 'CANC'
"                   WHERE zstatus = 'HOLD'.

followupAnswerBank.question: What are the common transport-related mistakes in Data Dictionary?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Common DDIC transport mistakes: Pehle, incomplete object collection — table ko bina uske dependent data elements ya domains ke transport karna. Transport target system mein arrive karta hai, activation fail hoti hai kyunki domain ya data element missing hai. Doosre, configuration tables ke liye, structure transport karna but table contents include nahi karna — business users ko production mein saari configuration manually re-enter karni padti hai. Teesre, wrong transport sequence — agar data element aur table separate transports mein hain aur table transport data element transport se pehle aata hai, activation fail hoti hai. SE01 use karke check karo ki saare dependent objects same transport mein hain ya correct order mein transported hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Common DDIC transport mistakes: First, incomplete object collection — transporting a table without its dependent data elements or domains. The transport arrives in target system, activation fails because domain or data element is missing. Second, for configuration tables, transporting the structure but not including the table contents — business users must manually re-enter all configuration in production. Third, wrong transport sequence — if data element and table are in separate transports and table transport arrives before data element transport, activation fails. Use SE01 to check all dependent objects are in the same transport or transported in correct order.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Go-live issue: Custom config table ZCFG_ROUTE transported to production. SE11 activation in production failed — error: domain ZROUTE_TYPE not found. Investigation: domain was in a different transport request that had not yet been imported. Emergency: domain transport imported manually, table activated. Delay of 2 hours at go-live. Prevention: use SE01 'Object List' check — verify all dependent objects in same transport.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Transport request mein sirf main object check karna bina dependencies verify kiye. DDIC objects have many dependencies — domains, data elements, tables are all linked. Use SE11 'Where-Used' and SE01 object list verification before releasing transport.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Check transport dependencies for a table:
" SE01 → Display → Object List → check all dependent objects

" ABAP: find transport request for an object
SELECT e070-trkorr, e071-object, e071-obj_name
  FROM e070 JOIN e071 ON e070~trkorr = e071~trkorr
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_tr)
  WHERE e071-obj_name = 'ZCUSTOM_TABLE'
    AND e070-trstatus = 'D'.  " D = modifiable

" Verify domain transported with table:
" Check e071 for both TABD (table) and DOMA (domain) entries
" in same transport request

followupAnswerBank.question: What should the enhancement category be set to in SE11 and why?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SE11 mein enhancement category define karta hai ki table ya structure future mein append structures ya customer includes ke saath extend hoga ya nahi aur kaise. Most custom tables ke liye recommended setting 'Can be enhanced (deep)' hai — yeh complex types including full append structures allow karta hai. Agar 'Cannot be enhanced' set hai, future mein koi append structure aapki table mein add nahi kar sakta — problem agar business requirements evolve karein. Agar enhancement category set nahi hai, SE11 warning dikhata hai aur certain operations fail ho sakti hain. Initial table creation ke dauran correctly set karna important hai: yeh affect karta hai ki dusre developers ke through ya SAP standard enhancements ke dauran table kaise extend ho sakti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Enhancement category in SE11 defines whether and how a table or structure can be extended with append structures or customer includes in the future. The recommended setting for most custom tables is 'Can be enhanced (deep)' — this allows full append structures including complex types. If set to 'Cannot be enhanced,' no one can add an append structure to your table in the future — a problem if business requirements evolve. If enhancement category is not set at all, SE11 shows a warning and certain operations may fail. Setting it correctly during initial table creation is important: it affects how the table can be extended by other developers or during SAP standard enhancements.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Team issue: Custom table ZCUSTOM_ORDER created without enhancement category set. Three months later, different team tried to add append structure — SE11 error: enhancement category not set. Table had to be modified to add enhancement category setting, transport released, and then append structure created. Additional transport, additional testing — avoidable with correct setup initially.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Enhancement category set karna bhool jaana when creating tables. SE11 deta hai warning but saves without it — easy to miss. Best practice: SE11 table creation mein enhancement category 'Can be enhanced (deep)' always set karo unless specific reason to restrict.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE11: Table Technical Settings
" Enhancement Category field:
" Value: 1 = Can be enhanced (deep)
"        2 = Can be enhanced (character-type or numeric)
"        3 = Cannot be enhanced
"        (blank) = Not classified - WARNING

" Check enhancement category of existing tables:
SELECT tabname, exclass
  FROM dd02l
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_enh)
  WHERE tabname LIKE 'Z%'
    AND as4local = 'A'
    AND exclass  = ' '.  " Find tables with no category set
" These tables need enhancement category assigned

data-dictionary-014.easyUnderstanding:
Data Dictionary changes production mein transport karne se pehle testing karna ek structured process hai — jaise ek doctor naya medicine prescribe karne se pehle trials karta hai.

Sabse pehla test: SE11 mein activate karo without errors. Ye basic sanity check hai — agar activate hi nahi hoti to aage kuch karne ki zaroorat nahi, pehle errors fix karo.

Doosra test: SE14 — database conversion tool. Naya field add kiya? SE14 se actual database table alter hoti hai. Existing data impact check hota hai. SE14 mein 'Check' button se pehle dekho kya hoga, phir 'Activate and Adjust Database' chalao.

Teesra: SE16N se data verify karo. Purani rows ko dekho — kya existing records me naya field empty hai ya galat value hai? Agar field mandatory hai to issue hoga.

Chautha: WHERE-USED check. Programs list dekho jo is table/data element use karte hain — un programs ko test karo manually.

Paanchwa: SM30 test agar Table Maintenance Generator use kiya hai. Naya data entry karo, existing edit karo, delete karo — sab operations test karo.

Chaatha: Foreign key test — invalid value enter karo, block hona chahiye. Valid value enter karo, accept hona chahiye.

Saatwa: Performance test agar index add kiya — ST05 se verify karo naya index use ho raha hai.

Aathwa: Transport completeness check — sab dependent objects ek saath transport mein hain.
data-dictionary-014.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Data Dictionary testing systematically 8 steps mein:

**Step 1: SE11 Activation Check**
- Table/view/data element/domain activate karo SE11 mein
- Zero activation errors hone chahiye
- Warnings review karo — kuch warnings production issues indicate karte hain
- Mass activation: SE11 → Edit → Activate All for related objects

**Step 2: SE14 Database Conversion**
- SE14: ABAP Dictionary → Database Utility
- 'Check' first — see what changes will be made without actually doing them
- 'Activate and Adjust Database' for actual conversion
- For field addition: conversion adds column to DB table
- For field deletion: WARNING — data loss
- Verify conversion completed without errors in log

**Step 3: SE16N Data Integrity Check**
- Open SE16N, enter table name
- Check existing records — new field default values correct?
- Existing data not corrupted?
- Count records before and after conversion — same count expected

**Step 4: WHERE-USED Impact Testing**
- SE11 WHERE-USED list — all programs using modified objects
- Execute each affected program in test system
- Check for runtime errors, wrong data, unexpected behavior
- Syntax check (SE38) is not enough — runtime test required

**Step 5: SM30 Maintenance View Test**
- Open SM30, enter view name
- Create new entry — all fields accept valid values?
- Edit existing entry — changes save correctly?
- Delete entry — referential checks work?
- Foreign key validation test — invalid value rejected?

**Step 6: Foreign Key Validation Test**
- Find a field with foreign key defined
- Via SM30 or custom program: enter invalid value (not in check table)
- SAP should reject with error message
- Enter valid value — should accept
- This confirms check table and join fields are correctly configured

**Step 7: Performance Testing for Indexes**
- ST05 SQL Trace: activate, run query, deactivate, display
- Check EXPLAIN column — does query use new index?
- HANA Studio or SAP HANA Cockpit: EXPLAIN PLAN for complex queries
- Index should show up in execution plan for WHERE clause fields

**Step 8: Transport Completeness Check**
- SE01: open transport request, check object list
- All dependent objects present: table + domain + data element + TMG programs
- Release transport in development
- Import in sandbox/QA — check SE11 activation in target system
- Only then transport to production
data-dictionary-014.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
"Testing Data Dictionary objects before production transport follows a structured eight-step approach. First, activate all objects in SE11 without errors — this is the baseline. Second, run SE14 database conversion after structural changes — verify the database adjustment completes successfully and existing data is intact. Third, use SE16N to confirm existing records are unaffected after conversion.

Fourth, run the WHERE-USED list from SE11 and execute all affected programs in the test system — syntax check alone is insufficient, runtime testing is required. Fifth, test SM30 maintenance if Table Maintenance Generator was used — verify create, change, and delete operations. Sixth, validate foreign key configuration by attempting to enter invalid values — they should be blocked. Seventh, if a new index was added, use ST05 SQL trace to confirm the query actually uses the new index. Eighth, verify transport completeness — all dependent objects in the same transport request before releasing to production.

Hinglish tip: Interview mein step-by-step testing approach bolo — interviewers ko structured answer pasand aata hai. 'I follow 8 steps before transporting any DDIC change' type opening strong impression deta hai."
data-dictionary-014.codeExamples:
* ST05 SQL trace verification for index usage
* SE16N data count check after conversion

" Step 3: SE16N data integrity - count records:
SELECT COUNT( * ) AS total_records
  FROM zcustom_table
  INTO @DATA(lv_total).
WRITE: / 'Total records:', lv_total.

" Step 6: Foreign key validation test in ABAP:
DATA ls_custom TYPE zcustom_table.
ls_custom-mandt   = sy-mandt.
ls_custom-zkey    = 'TEST001'.
ls_custom-zmatnr  = 'INVALID_MAT'.  " Not in MARA
INSERT zcustom_table FROM ls_custom.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  WRITE: / 'Foreign key blocked invalid entry - correct!'.
ENDIF.

" Step 7: Check if index exists and is active:
SELECT tabname, indexname, dbindex
  FROM dd12l
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_idx)
  WHERE tabname   = 'ZCUSTOM_TABLE'
    AND as4local  = 'A'
    AND dbindex   = 'X'.  " X = index active in DB

Second, run SE14 database conversion after structural changes — verify the database adjustment completes successfully and existing data is intact. Third, use SE16N to confirm existing records are unaffected after conversion.

Fourth, run the WHERE-USED list from SE11 and execute all affected programs in the test system — syntax check alone is insufficient, runtime testing is required. Fifth, test SM30 maintenance if Table Maintenance Generator was used — verify create, change, and delete operations. Sixth, validate foreign key configuration by attempting to enter invalid values — they should be blocked. Seventh, if a new index was added, use ST05 SQL trace to confirm the query actually uses the new index. Eighth, verify transport completeness — all dependent objects in the same transport request before releasing to production.
data-dictionary-014.interviewScriptHinglish:
production transport se pehle Data Dictionary objects ka testing ek structured eight-step approach follow karta hai. Pehla, SE11 mein saare objects ko errors ke bina activate karo — yeh baseline hai. Doosra, structural changes ke baad SE14 database conversion run karo — verify karo database adjustment successfully complete ho aur existing data intact ho. Teesra, SE16N use karo confirm karne ke liye ki existing records conversion ke baad unaffected hain.

Chautha, SE11 se WHERE-USED list run karo aur test system mein saare affected programs execute karo — syntax check akela insufficient hai, runtime testing chahiye. Paancha, Table Maintenance Generator use hua tha toh SM30 maintenance test karo — create, change, aur delete operations verify karo. Chhata, invalid values enter karke foreign key configuration validate karo — woh block hone chahiye. Saatva, naya index add hua tha toh ST05 SQL trace se confirm karo query actually naya index use kar rahi hai. Aatva, transport completeness verify karo — production release se pehle saare dependent objects same transport request mein hone chahiye.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Galti: SE14 'Activate and Adjust' directly run karna without 'Check' first.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Check step se pata chalta hai agar koi destructive operation hone wala hai — data drop, type conversion issue. Always Check before Adjust.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Galti: WHERE-USED list dekh ke sab programs SE38 mein syntax check karna aur assume karna sab theek hai.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Syntax check runtime errors nahi pakad sakta. Dynamic code, CASE statements, IF conditions — runtime test zaroori hai.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Galti: Foreign key define karna aur assume karna validation automatically enforced hai.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: SE11 mein 'Check Required' checkbox explicitly enable karna zaroori hai for hard validation. Otherwise foreign key is documentation only — no runtime enforcement.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Galti: Index create karke assume karna automatically use hoga.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Always verify with ST05 that the query actually uses the new index. Common reason for index not used: WHERE clause fields do not match index fields exactly, or query has too many result rows (optimizer skips index for large result sets).

data-dictionary-014.quickRevisionNotes:
What: DDIC change testing combines row counts, foreign key validation, index verification, and SQL trace to confirm transport-safe behavior before promoting to QAS/PRD.

Used for:
- Validating SE14 table conversion preserved all rows
- Confirming foreign key constraints block invalid inserts
- Verifying new secondary indexes are active and used in queries

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use SE14 and what is the difference between Check and Adjust Database?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SE14 ABAP Dictionary Database Utility hai jo ABAP Dictionary definition ko physical database table ke saath synchronize karta hai. Structural changes ke baad use karo: fields add karna, field order change karna, ya technical properties adjust karna. 'Check' function DDIC definition aur actual database ke beech difference analyze karta hai bina changes kiye — dikhata hai kya hoga. Data loss warnings ya conversion issues ke liye check output review karo. 'Activate and Adjust Database' function actual database ALTER statement perform karta hai — new columns add karta hai, types adjust karta hai. Hamesha pehle Check run karo, output review karo, phir Adjust run karo. Adjust ke baad, verify karo ki log no errors ke saath successful completion dikhata hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SE14 is the ABAP Dictionary Database Utility that synchronizes the ABAP Dictionary definition with the physical database table. Use it after structural changes: adding fields, changing field order, or adjusting technical properties. The 'Check' function analyzes the difference between DDIC definition and actual database without making any changes — it shows what will happen. Review the check output for any data loss warnings or conversion issues. The 'Activate and Adjust Database' function performs the actual database ALTER statement — adds new columns, adjusts types. Always run Check first, review output, then run Adjust. After Adjust, verify the log shows successful completion with no errors.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: SE14 Check output showed: 'Field ZOLD_FIELD exists in database but not in DDIC — will be dropped.' Developer had removed a field from DDIC definition but forgot there was still data in that column in the database. SE14 Check caught this before Adjust was run. Field was reinstated in DDIC, data migrated to new field, then old field removed safely.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: SE14 'Activate and Adjust' directly run karna without 'Check' first. Check step se pata chalta hai agar koi destructive operation hone wala hai — data drop, type conversion issue. Always Check before Adjust.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE14: ABAP Dictionary Utilities → Database Utility
" Object type: Database tables
" Enter table name: ZCUSTOM_TABLE
" Action 1: Check → review output, no errors expected
" Action 2: Activate and Adjust Database → actual change

" Verify table structure in DB after SE14:
SELECT fieldname, leng, inttype
  FROM dd03l
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_fields)
  WHERE tabname  = 'ZCUSTOM_TABLE'
    AND as4local = 'A'
  ORDER BY position.
" Compare expected fields with actual DDIC definition

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the WHERE-USED check in SE11 and how do you use it for testing?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SE11 mein WHERE-USED un saare objects ki complete list generate karta hai jo given DDIC object ko reference karte hain — modified table, data element, ya domain use karne wale programs, function modules, classes, dusri tables, structures, aur views. Testing ke liye, yeh list aapka test scope hai: DDIC change ke baad test system mein WHERE-USED result mein har program ko test karna padega. Representative data ke saath har program run karo aur verify karo ki output correct hai. Special attention domain fixed values check karne wale programs par — agar aapne domain values modify kiye, yeh programs differently behave kar sakte hain. WHERE-USED SE11 menu ke through ya SE80 mein Ctrl+Shift+F3 ke through access hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: WHERE-USED in SE11 generates a complete list of all objects that reference a given DDIC object — programs, function modules, classes, other tables, structures, and views that use the modified table, data element, or domain. For testing, this list is your test scope: every program in the WHERE-USED result must be tested in the test system after the DDIC change. Run each program with representative data and verify output is correct. Special attention to programs that check domain fixed values — if you modified domain values, these programs may behave differently. WHERE-USED accessed via SE11 menu or Ctrl+Shift+F3 in SE80.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Domain value added for new order status 'PART' (partial delivery). WHERE-USED on domain ZORDER_STATUS showed 12 programs. Tested all 12 — 11 worked fine. 12th program had a CASE statement: WHEN 'COMP' WHEN 'CANC' WHEN OTHERS... — the OTHERS branch handled 'PART' but with wrong logic. Bug found through WHERE-USED-driven testing before production transport.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: WHERE-USED list dekh ke sab programs SE38 mein syntax check karna aur assume karna sab theek hai. Syntax check runtime errors nahi pakad sakta. Dynamic code, CASE statements, IF conditions — runtime test zaroori hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE11 WHERE-USED: Menu → Where-Used List
" For domain ZORDER_STATUS:
" Programs using domain (direct or via data element)
" Structures referencing data element
" Tables with fields using data element

" Testing each program from WHERE-USED list:
" 1. SE38: open program
" 2. F8 (Execute) with test data
" 3. Check for runtime errors, wrong results
" 4. Document test result

" Quick check: programs using a specific table
SELECT progname, include
  FROM d010tab
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_progs)
  WHERE tabname = 'ZCUSTOM_TABLE'.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test foreign key validation after setting up a foreign key in SE11?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Foreign key validation test karne ke liye teen scenarios chahiye. Pehle, valid value ke saath test — value check table ke key field mein exist karni chahiye. Entry bina error accept honi chahiye. Doosre, invalid value ke saath test — woh value jo check table mein exist nahi karti. SAP foreign key validation error message ke saath entry reject kare. Teesre, foreign key field par F4 help test karo — automatically join condition ke through filtered check table se values dikhane chahiye. Agar valid values reject ho rahi hain, foreign key definition mein check table ya join condition galat hai. Agar invalid values accept ho rahi hain, foreign key active nahi hai ya field correctly configured nahi hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Testing foreign key validation requires three scenarios. First, test with a valid value — the value must exist in the check table's key field. The entry should be accepted without error. Second, test with an invalid value — a value that does not exist in the check table. SAP should reject the entry with a foreign key validation error message. Third, test F4 help on the foreign key field — it should automatically show values from the check table, filtered by the join condition. If valid values are rejected, the check table or join condition in the foreign key definition is wrong. If invalid values are accepted, the foreign key may not be active or the field is not correctly configured.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Foreign key testing found: custom table ZCUSTOM_PLANT had foreign key on WERKS field pointing to T001W. Test with valid plant '1000' — accepted. Test with invalid 'XXXX' — also accepted! Investigation: foreign key was defined but 'Check Required' checkbox was unchecked in SE11 — foreign key was advisory only, not enforced. Fixed: enabled Check Required — retested, invalid value now blocked correctly.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Foreign key define karna aur assume karna validation automatically enforced hai. SE11 mein 'Check Required' checkbox explicitly enable karna zaroori hai for hard validation. Otherwise foreign key is documentation only — no runtime enforcement.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Foreign key test via ABAP (simulates SM30 validation):
DATA ls_test TYPE zcustom_plant.
ls_test-mandt = sy-mandt.
ls_test-zkey  = 'TEST'.

" Test 1: Valid plant
ls_test-werks = '1000'.  " Exists in T001W
INSERT zcustom_plant FROM ls_test.
WRITE: / 'Valid plant result:', sy-subrc.  " Expect 0

" Test 2: Invalid plant
ls_test-werks = 'XXXX'.  " Not in T001W
INSERT zcustom_plant FROM ls_test.
WRITE: / 'Invalid plant result:', sy-subrc.  " Expect non-0
ROLLBACK WORK.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you verify that a new secondary index is being used by queries using ST05?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ST05 SQL Trace ABAP application server aur database ke beech saare SQL statements record karta hai. Index usage verify karne ke liye: ST05 trace activate karo, target query run karne wala program ya transaction execute karo, trace deactivate karo, trace display karo. Trace list mein relevant SQL statement dhundo. Explain click karo — ST05 database execution plan dikhata hai. Execution plan mein, ACCESS METHOD column mein index name dhundo. Agar index appear karta hai, woh use ho raha hai. Agar FULL TABLE SCAN ya no index reference dikhta hai, ya toh query ka WHERE clause index fields ke saath match nahi karta, index statistics outdated hain, ya optimizer ne index use nahi karne ka decide kiya. More detailed HANA-specific analysis ke liye HANA Studio EXPLAIN PLAN bhi check karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: ST05 SQL Trace records all SQL statements between ABAP application server and the database. To verify index usage: activate ST05 trace, execute the program or transaction that runs the target query, deactivate trace, display trace. Find the relevant SQL statement in the trace list. Click Explain — ST05 shows the database execution plan. In the execution plan, look for the index name in the ACCESS METHOD column. If the index appears, it is being used. If you see FULL TABLE SCAN or no index reference, either the query's WHERE clause does not match the index fields, the index statistics are outdated, or the optimizer chose not to use the index. Also check HANA Studio EXPLAIN PLAN for more detailed HANA-specific analysis.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Index added on ZCUSTOM_ORDER fields (BUKRS, ERDAT) to speed up a report. ST05 trace on the report showed: query accessing ZCUSTOM_ORDER but execution plan showed full column scan, index not used. Investigation: report WHERE clause had BUKRS and BLDAT — but index was on BUKRS and ERDAT. Wrong field in index. Index rebuilt with correct fields (BUKRS, BLDAT). ST05 rerun — index now appeared in execution plan, report time dropped 70%.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Index create karke assume karna automatically use hoga. Always verify with ST05 that the query actually uses the new index. Common reason for index not used: WHERE clause fields do not match index fields exactly, or query has too many result rows (optimizer skips index for large result sets).
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ST05: SQL Trace steps
" 1. Transaction ST05 → Activate Trace
" 2. Run target program/transaction
" 3. ST05 → Deactivate Trace
" 4. ST05 → Display Trace
" 5. Find target SQL → click Explain

" Verify index in ABAP (check index exists and is DB-active):
SELECT a~tabname, a~indexname, a~dbindex,
       b~fieldname, b~position
  FROM dd12l AS a
  JOIN dd17s AS b
    ON a~tabname    = b~tabname
   AND a~indexname  = b~indexname
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_idx)
  WHERE a~tabname   = 'ZCUSTOM_ORDER'
    AND a~as4local  = 'A'
    AND a~dbindex   = 'X'.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test SM30 table maintenance after Table Maintenance Generator is run?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SM30 testing full CRUD operations cover karta hai: Create — saare required fields aur valid values ke saath new record enter karo, Save click karo, verify karo ki record table mein appear ho. Read — display mode existing records correctly dikhata hai. Update — existing record select karo, Change click karo, field modify karo, save karo, verify karo ki change persist hua. Delete — record select karo, Delete click karo, confirm karo, verify karo ki record remove ho gaya. Additionally validation test karo: domain-restricted field ke liye invalid value enter karo — reject hona chahiye. Foreign key field ke liye check table mein nahi hai aisi value enter karo — reject hona chahiye. Authorization test karo: agar authorization group set hai, authorization na rakhne wale user ke saath test karo — SM30 access deny kare. Finally, TMG-generated programs mein add kiye gaye koi custom enhancements ya user exits test karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SM30 testing covers full CRUD operations: Create — enter a new record with all required fields and valid values, click Save, verify record appears in table. Read — display mode shows existing records correctly. Update — select existing record, click Change, modify a field, save, verify change persisted. Delete — select a record, click Delete, confirm, verify record removed. Additionally test validation: enter an invalid value for a domain-restricted field — should be rejected. Enter a value not in the check table for a foreign key field — should be rejected. Test authorization: if authorization group is set, test with a user who lacks the authorization — SM30 should deny access. Finally, test any custom enhancements or user exits added to the TMG-generated programs.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: UAT testing of SM30 for ZCFG_PRICE maintenance: Create test — passed. Update test — passed. Delete test — failed with 'Deletion not allowed' error. Investigation: TMG was generated with 'Maintenance Type: Display and Change' but not 'Full Maintenance (with delete)'. Regenerated TMG with correct maintenance type. Delete test passed. UAT issue found in testing — not in production. Importance of comprehensive SM30 testing demonstrated.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: SM30 mein sirf create aur display test karna. Delete operation, authorization test, validation test — sab required hain. TMG maintenance type settings directly affect what operations are allowed — test all operations.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SM30 Test Checklist:
" 1. SM30 → Table: ZCFG_PRICE → Maintain
" 2. New Entries → fill all fields → Save → verify in list
" 3. Select record → Change → modify value → Save → verify
" 4. Select record → Delete → confirm → verify removed
" 5. New entry with invalid MATNR → should get FK error
" 6. Wrong user test → should get auth error

" Verify TMG programs exist (created by generator):
SELECT funcname, include
  FROM tfdir
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_func)
  WHERE pname = 'ZFG_ZCFG_PRICE'.  " TMG function group
" Should show generated maintenance function modules

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you verify that a transport includes all necessary DDIC dependencies before releasing?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Transport completeness verify karne ke liye SE01 ya SE10 mein object list review karna padta hai. Transport request open karo, Object List tab check karo — saare DDIC objects aur unki dependencies listed hone chahiye: table (TABD), domain (DOMA), data element (DTEL), aur TMG-generated programs (function group FUGR, function modules FUNC, screens DYNP). Practical verification approach: pehle transport ko sandbox ya lower client mein import karo aur SE11 mein activation try karo. Agar activation 'object not found' errors ke saath fail hoti hai, missing objects identify karo aur transport mein add karo. Sirf lower system mein successful activation ke baad production mein release karo. Object completeness verify karne ke liye release se pehle SE11 'consistency check' bhi use karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Verifying transport completeness requires reviewing the object list in SE01 or SE10. Open the transport request, check the Object List tab — all DDIC objects and their dependencies must be listed: table (TABD), domain (DOMA), data element (DTEL), and TMG-generated programs (function group FUGR, function modules FUNC, screens DYNP). A practical verification approach: import the transport into a sandbox or lower client first and attempt activation in SE11 there. If activation fails with 'object not found' errors, identify the missing objects and add them to the transport. Only release to production after successful activation in a lower system. Also use SE11 'consistency check' to verify object completeness before release.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Pre-production checklist: transport request had TABD (table), DTEL (data element) but DOMA (domain) was missing — it was in a separate older transport already released. The domain existed in QA from a previous import but team was not sure about production. Solution: checked production SE11 — domain not found. Added domain to a new transport, imported domain first, then table transport. Activation in production successful. Without this check, production go-live would have failed.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: 'Domain pehle se production mein hogi' assume karna bina verify kiye. Always explicitly check target system SE11 for all dependent objects. Never assume — verify each dependency in the actual target system.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE01/SE10: Check transport object list
" TABD = Table definition
" DOMA = Domain
" DTEL = Data element
" FUGR = Function group (TMG programs)
" DYNP = Screen (TMG maintenance screen)

" Verify dependent objects in target system:
" SE11 in target system → check domain exists:
SELECT domname, as4local
  FROM dd01l
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_dom)
  WHERE domname  = 'ZSTATUS_DOMAIN'
    AND as4local = 'A'.  " Must return 1 row
" Empty result = domain missing in target → transport it first

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the process of testing a new custom table end-to-end before production transport?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: New custom table ke liye end-to-end testing: Step one, SE11 activation bina errors. Step two, SE14 check followed by Activate and Adjust Database — verify karo ki new table database mein appear hoti hai. Step three, SE16N — table open karo, verify karo ki structure correctly display hota hai. Step four, agar TMG generate hua, full SM30 CRUD test run karo. Step five, domain aur data element test karo — F4 help correct values dikhata hai, field labels saari column header lengths mein correctly display hote hain. Step six, valid aur invalid values ke saath foreign key validation test. Step seven, table par INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE cover karne wala chhota test ABAP program likho — verify karo no runtime issues. Step eight, sandbox mein transport karo, sandbox mein SE11 mein activate karo, smoke test run karo — sirf phir QA aur production mein release karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: End-to-end testing for a new custom table: Step one, SE11 activation without errors. Step two, SE14 check followed by Activate and Adjust Database — verify new table appears in database. Step three, SE16N — open table, verify structure displays correctly. Step four, if TMG was generated, run full SM30 CRUD test. Step five, test domain and data element — F4 help shows correct values, field labels display correctly in all column header lengths. Step six, foreign key validation test with valid and invalid values. Step seven, write a small test ABAP program covering INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE on the table — verify no runtime issues. Step eight, transport to sandbox, activate in SE11 in sandbox, run smoke test — only then release to QA and production.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: New table ZCUSTOM_ROUTE created for logistics project. Followed complete test process. Found during SE14 check: table name exceeded 16-character limit for one specific database — SE14 warning. Table renamed before going further. Found during SM30 test: authorization group was left blank — any user could maintain. Authorization group added. Found during ABAP test: field ZROUTE_ID defined as NUMC but test INSERT used CHAR value — type mismatch. All 3 issues found in development, none reached production.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: New table create karke direct production transport karna without end-to-end test in development. Each testing step can catch different issues — activation catches DDIC errors, SE14 catches DB issues, SM30 tests functional correctness, ABAP test catches type mismatches. All steps are needed.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle testing when a DDIC change affects existing production data?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab DDIC change existing production data affect karti hai, testing mein data impact analysis include hona chahiye. Production se representative data sample test system mein export karo — SE16N export ya data copy tool use karo. Iss sample ke against structural change test karo: SE14 conversion run karo aur verify karo no data loss ya truncation errors. New mandatory fields ke liye, woh data migration script likho aur test karo jo existing records ke liye default values populate karega. Production data sample ke against test system mein migration script test karo — verify karo ki row counts pehle aur baad mein match karte hain. Rollback plan create karo: production issues aaye toh change ko reverse kaise karein. Badi tables ke liye, running processes par impact minimize karne ke liye SE14 conversion ke liye maintenance window plan karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a DDIC change affects existing production data, testing must include data impact analysis. Export a representative data sample from production to the test system — use SE16N export or a data copy tool. Test the structural change against this sample: run SE14 conversion and verify no data loss or truncation errors. For new mandatory fields, write and test the data migration script that will populate default values for existing records. Test the migration script in the test system against the production data sample — verify row counts match before and after. Create a rollback plan: how to reverse the change if production issues occur. For large tables, plan a maintenance window for SE14 conversion to minimize impact on running processes.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Field ZDESCRIPTION extended from CHAR 40 to CHAR 60 on ZCUSTOM_PRODUCT (50,000 records). Production data sample imported to dev. SE14 on dev sample: successful — no data truncation (extension only). Migration: no script needed, extension does not affect existing data. Rollback plan: if needed, reduce back to 40 (no data truncation since existing values ≤40). Production transport during off-peak hours. SE14 in production: 23 seconds for 50,000 records. Zero impact on users.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Production data impact test karo bina production ke realistic data volume se. Dev system mein 100 test records ho sakti hain — production mein 5 million. SE14 conversion time dev mein 1 second, production mein 45 minutes — users affected during conversion window. Always estimate production conversion time using production data volume.
